thesis: GRASIM shows strong analyst-revision momentum and is a liquid large/mid diversified name; upsizing it improves bet quality without adding small-cap beta.
falsification: Revisions roll over and the stock lags NIFTY 500 over the next quarter, or cement/VSF margins disappoint.

thesis: Strong, rising analyst revisions on a scaling consumer-retail franchise justify an overweight beyond the baseline's flat sizing.
falsification: Revisions fade, growth/margin guidance is cut, or the name underperforms the consumer basket over the next 1-2 quarters.

thesis: Strong analyst revisions plus above-peer cheapness on a large, liquid energy major give a de-correlated, capacity-friendly bet that tempers book beta.
falsification: Crude/realisation weakness drives revisions down, or ONGC lags the energy basket over the next quarter.

thesis: Rising analyst revisions on a large, under-owned life insurer offer a liquid, de-correlated financials bet to broaden the book.
falsification: Revisions stall, VNB/margin trends disappoint, or LICI underperforms SBILIFE/insurers over the next quarter.

thesis: Strong analyst-revision momentum on a high-ROE gold-financier adds quality financials breadth distinct from the book's banks.
falsification: Gold-price reversal or regulatory tightening turns revisions down and the stock lags financials.

thesis: COFORGE carries strong, rising revisions against a generally weak-revision IT pack, making it the sector's best risk-adjusted exposure.
falsification: IT-spend softness drags revisions down or COFORGE underperforms TECHM/PERSISTENT over the next quarter.

thesis: Strong analyst revisions plus stable utility cash flows give a defensive, de-correlated addition.
falsification: Gas-spread/volume pressure turns revisions down, or the name lags the energy/utility basket.

thesis: Strong revisions and above-peer cheapness on a liquid iron-ore producer add capacity-friendly materials breadth.
falsification: Iron-ore price weakness turns revisions down or NMDC lags the materials basket over the next quarter.

thesis: Strong analyst-revision momentum signals improving asset-quality/growth at a beaten-down SFB.
falsification: Asset-quality stress or NIM compression turns revisions down and the stock lags SFB peers.

thesis: Strong revisions on an injectables CDMO add quality healthcare breadth distinct from existing small-cap pharma.
falsification: US-generic pricing or regulatory issues turn revisions down, or GLAND lags the pharma basket.

thesis: Strong analyst revisions on a defence-explosives compounder add a de-correlated structural-growth bet.
falsification: Order-flow/margin disappointment turns revisions down or the stock lags industrials over the quarter.

thesis: Rising analyst revisions on a liquid generics major add capacity-friendly healthcare breadth.
falsification: US-pipeline or pricing setbacks turn revisions down or the name lags pharma peers.

thesis: Holding a strong-revision MFI keeps quality financials breadth as the credit cycle normalises.
falsification: MFI asset-quality stress turns revisions down and the stock lags financials.

thesis: Pipe/industrial order momentum justifies a position but no longer a top weight; reduce concentration.
falsification: Order intake re-accelerates and revisions re-strengthen, making the trim premature.

thesis: Aluminium exposure is worth holding as a cyclical but revisions no longer support a top weight.
falsification: Aluminium prices and revisions strengthen, making the trim premature.

thesis: Wires-and-cables demand supports a holding, sized down to manage small/mid beta and capacity.
falsification: Revisions re-accelerate and the stock outperforms industrials, making the trim premature.

thesis: Forgings cyclical worth holding but revisions no longer justify a top-tier weight.
falsification: Defence/export order momentum lifts revisions and the stock outperforms auto-components.

thesis: Retail-broking/structural capital-markets growth justifies a core weight despite cooling revisions.
falsification: Cyclical drop in cash-market volumes turns revisions down and the stock lags capital-markets peers.

thesis: Electrification/capex tailwind supports holding despite revisions cooling to neutral.
falsification: Order-book or margin disappointment turns revisions down and the stock lags industrials.

thesis: Powergen/data-centre demand supports a core holding even with neutral revisions.
falsification: Demand softens and revisions fall, with the stock lagging industrials.

thesis: Strong CDMO order momentum and firm revisions support a core healthcare weight.

thesis: Structural CDMO growth justifies a holding, sized down as revisions normalise.
falsification: Revisions re-strengthen and the stock outperforms pharma, making the de-size premature.

thesis: E-commerce share gains support holding through near-term revision noise.

thesis: Digital-engineering growth supports a holding despite cooling revisions.

thesis: 2-wheeler/export franchise worth holding, sized down as revisions soften.

thesis: EV-2W structural optionality is worth holding but at a reduced, risk-controlled weight.
falsification: Volume/margin inflection lifts revisions and it outperforms autos, making the trim premature.

thesis: CV-systems franchise worth a smaller holding as revisions cool.
falsification: CV-cycle upturn lifts revisions and it outperforms auto-components.

thesis: Grasim's VSF/chemicals/cement plus the Birla paints optionality is a quality compounder, and rising revisions confirm the earnings path; size up modestly from 2.09%.
falsification: Revisions roll over and the paints venture cash-burn drags consolidated margins, or the stock underperforms NIFTY 500 by >8% over 3 months.

thesis: Dominant eyewear platform with rising analyst optimism; a structural consumer-discretionary compounder I want to own through the cycle.
falsification: Same-store growth decelerates and revisions fall, or competitive intensity compresses unit economics; underperformance >10% in 3 months.

thesis: CreditAccess Grameen has improving asset-quality signals and rising revisions as the rural MFI cycle turns; hold a meaningful weight.
falsification: Rural delinquencies spike or revisions turn down, signaling the MFI credit cycle rolling over.

thesis: Asian Paints is a dominant brand where revisions are stabilizing higher; a defensive quality holding to balance the cyclicals.
falsification: Margin pressure from new entrants persists and revisions fall back below neutral.

thesis: ONGC carries strong analyst-revision momentum and trades modestly cheap to peers; a liquid large-cap that diversifies the book away from high-beta cyclicals.
falsification: Crude weakens sharply or revisions reverse, removing the earnings tailwind.

thesis: LIC's VNB trajectory is improving with rising revisions; a liquid, low-beta financial that broadens the insurance sleeve.
falsification: Embedded-value growth stalls or revisions fall, or the stock lags financials by >8% in 3 months.

thesis: Coforge has rising analyst optimism and a strong deal pipeline; a way to own IT-services growth where revisions are clearly positive rather than the flat-revision large caps.
falsification: Deal momentum slows, revisions turn down, or discretionary IT spend contracts.

thesis: Muthoot Finance has rising revisions as gold-loan AUM and yields stay strong; a liquid mid-cap financial diversifier.
falsification: Gold prices fall sharply or regulatory LTV tightening hits AUM, and revisions reverse.

thesis: Gland Pharma's revisions are firming as the injectables pipeline and US base business recover; a mid-cap healthcare diversifier.
falsification: US pricing pressure or a regulatory observation derails the recovery and revisions fall.

thesis: Anthem Biosciences rides the specialty-chemistry/CRDMO outsourcing theme with still-positive revisions; hold core.
falsification: Order book softens and revisions turn negative.

thesis: Sai Life retains a structural CDMO story but analyst optimism has faded to neutral, so I reduce size while keeping a toehold.
falsification: Revisions re-accelerate and the order book inflects up, in which case the trim was wrong.

thesis: CG Power is a structural beneficiary of India's electrification and capex cycle; hold despite revisions cooling to neutral.
falsification: Order inflows slow and revisions turn clearly negative.

thesis: Cummins India is a high-ROCE industrial leveraged to data-center and infra power demand; hold core.
falsification: Domestic capex stalls or export demand weakens with revisions turning down.

thesis: Bharat Forge keeps defense/export optionality but it is a high-beta cyclical with only modestly positive revisions, so I reduce to manage book beta.
falsification: Defense order wins accelerate and revisions strengthen, making the trim premature.

thesis: RR Kabel benefits from the cables capex theme but revisions are only mildly positive; cut the oversized 2.44% toward a normal weight.
falsification: Margin expansion and revisions accelerate, showing I trimmed a winner too early.

thesis: Hindalco/Novelis has decent aluminium fundamentals but is a high-beta commodity name with only neutral revisions; trim to lower book beta.
falsification: Aluminium and Novelis spreads widen with revisions improving.

thesis: Welspun Corp has a strong order book but is a lumpy small-cap cyclical with neutral revisions; reduce from the top weight to control concentration.
falsification: Large new pipeline orders land and revisions inflect higher.

thesis: Axis Bank is a core private-sector lender with adequate revisions; hold as part of a balanced bank sleeve.
falsification: Asset-quality slippage or NIM compression with revisions falling.

thesis: ICICI Bank is the highest-quality large private lender; hold as the anchor of the bank sleeve.
falsification: Credit costs rise materially or growth stalls relative to peers.

thesis: State Bank offers cheap exposure to the credit cycle with strong franchise scale; hold a core weight.
falsification: Slippages rise or the cheapness fails to re-rate as revisions keep falling.

thesis: HDFC Bank's deposit-led re-rating remains intact; hold as a low-beta financial anchor.
falsification: Deposit growth or margins disappoint and the stock keeps lagging the sector.

thesis: Kotak is a conservative, high-quality lender; hold a modest core weight.
falsification: Growth continues to lag peers with revisions deteriorating.

thesis: AU SFB is a fast-growing franchise transitioning toward universal-bank scale; hold core.
falsification: Credit costs rise or the growth premium compresses as revisions fall.

thesis: Federal Bank is a steady mid-cap lender but revisions have eased; modest trim while keeping exposure.
falsification: NIM and growth re-accelerate with revisions improving.

thesis: Shriram Finance has a strong rural NBFC franchise but revisions are clearly softening; reduce while holding a position.
falsification: AUM growth and asset quality surprise positively and revisions turn up.

thesis: Cholamandalam is a best-in-class vehicle/SME lender with steady revisions; hold core.
falsification: Asset-quality deterioration or sharp growth slowdown with revisions falling.

thesis: Apollo Hospitals is a structural healthcare-delivery and pharmacy/digital compounder; hold.
falsification: Occupancy or ARPOB growth stalls and the digital losses widen.

thesis: Dr Lal PathLabs is a high-ROCE diagnostics franchise; hold a core weight.
falsification: Pricing competition from online players compresses margins and revisions fall.

thesis: Sun Pharma's specialty (derma/onco) franchise underpins durable growth; hold core healthcare.
falsification: Specialty ramp disappoints or a major regulatory issue emerges.

thesis: Mankind Pharma has a strong domestic branded-generics and consumer-health franchise; hold core.

thesis: Avenue Supermarts remains the most efficient grocery retailer with a long runway; hold despite neutral revisions.

thesis: Trent's Zudio-led store expansion is a structural discretionary-retail compounder; hold core.

thesis: Meesho is a high-growth value e-commerce platform but path-to-profit is unproven and revisions are only neutral; reduce from 1.85%.

thesis: Eternal (Zomato/Blinkit) is the leader in quick-commerce, a large structural opportunity; hold a modest position given path-to-profit risk.

thesis: Mahindra's SUV momentum and farm-equipment leadership make it a quality cyclical; hold.
